narrative:
Skies are partly sunny today with warm temperatures. You will also notice a bump-up in humidity levels after enjoying a few lower-dewpoint days. An afternoon shower or thundershower will be dotted around the mountains, For the weekend, hit-or-miss showers and thunderstorms will be around, focused on the afternoon, but not necessarily confined to it. Next week, we begin to heat up and dry out.

Warm; PM Thunder Around

Last Updated Fri June 26 at 3:08PM by Scott Stephens

Skies are partly sunny today with warm temperatures. You will also notice a bump-up in humidity levels after enjoying a few lower-dewpoint days. An afternoon shower or thundershower will be dotted around the mountains, For the weekend, hit-or-miss showers and thunderstorms will be around, focused on the afternoon, but not necessarily confined to it. Next week, we begin to heat up and dry out.

Forecast Discussion

A cold front across the Ohio Valley today will get close enough to encourage afternoon showers and thundershowers today. Temperatures will be warm, and we are also embarking on a more humid period that will last for some time.

The front should drift south into the NC/VA border by late weekend or early next week before getting repelled northeastward by a building dome of high pressure over the Mid-South.

This developing hot dome will produce the season’s hottest weather-to-date during the run-up to Independence Day. With this large upper anticyclone in place, it will be suppress afternoon shower and thunderstorm activity going into the middle part of next week.

Anytime you have this kind of heat, a rogue thundershower is never completely off the table. But after Tuesday, the extended part of this forecast will be mostly hot and dry.

The heat ridge will probably break down after the Fourth, and that may also be accompanied by better opportunities for afternoon showers and thundershowers.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

WWR? The Blowing Rock

Jun 25, 2026
Monday Message for June 22, 2026

The week starts stormy, but ends up like the first full week of summer should, with hit-or-miss afternoon thundershowers and very warm temperatures. It's the Monday Message, available for our subscribers.

Jun 22, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.
